jtbds.title: Admit a patient for inpatient care
jtbds.situation: patient presents for scheduled or emergency inpatient admission; coordinator must verify benefits, obtain authorization, assign bed, and complete consents
jtbds.motivation: ensure patient receives timely care with coverage verified to prevent revenue denials
jtbds.outcome: patient admitted with signed consents, authorization number obtained, bed assigned
success_criteria: insurance verified and authorization obtained before or within 24 hours of emergency admission
signed consent forms collected before first procedure
bed assigned within 2 hours of admission order
HIPAA notice of privacy practices delivered and acknowledged

jtbds.title: Schedule a patient appointment
jtbds.situation: patient or referring provider requests an appointment; coordinator must check availability, verify insurance eligibility, and confirm scheduling
jtbds.motivation: minimize patient wait time and maximize provider schedule utilization while ensuring coverage is active
jtbds.outcome: appointment confirmed, eligibility verified, reminders sent to patient
success_criteria: appointment offered within 7 days for routine, 24 hours for urgent
insurance eligibility verified at time of scheduling
appointment reminder sent 48 hours and 2 hours before visit
no-show rate < 10%

jtbds.title: Process prior authorization request
jtbds.situation: payer requires authorization before procedure, medication, or specialist visit; specialist must submit clinical criteria and obtain approval before service rendered
jtbds.motivation: prevent claim denials by obtaining payer approval in advance, while minimizing treatment delays for patients
jtbds.outcome: authorization approved or denied, appeal initiated if denied, authorization number documented in chart
success_criteria: initial authorization request submitted within 24 hours of order
urgent authorization escalated to payer within 4 hours
appeal filed within 30 days of denial
authorization number entered in scheduling system before procedure

jtbds.title: Submit and follow up on insurance claim
jtbds.situation: services rendered; billing specialist must code the encounter, validate claim data, submit electronically to payer, and follow up on unpaid or rejected claims
jtbds.motivation: maximize clean claim rate and collect accounts receivable within payer timely filing limits
jtbds.outcome: claim paid, posted to patient account, and ERA reconciled; denials worked or appealed
success_criteria: claim submitted within 3 business days of service date
clean claim rate > 95%
denial worked within 10 business days of receipt
timely filing limit met for all payers

jtbds.title: Manage specialist referral
jtbds.situation: attending physician orders a specialist referral; coordinator must obtain authorization, identify in-network specialist, transmit clinical summary, and track appointment completion
jtbds.motivation: ensure continuity of care and avoid out-of-network liability while meeting the physician's clinical timeline
jtbds.outcome: referral authorized, specialist appointment scheduled, consult note received back in chart
success_criteria: referral authorization requested within 24 hours of order
specialist appointment scheduled within 5 business days of authorization
consult note received and scanned within 3 business days of specialist visit
referring physician notified of consult findings

jtbds.title: Coordinate patient discharge
jtbds.situation: attending physician writes discharge order; case manager must coordinate post-acute placement, arrange follow-up, reconcile medications, and obtain patient education acknowledgement
jtbds.motivation: prevent avoidable readmissions and ensure patient has safe, appropriate discharge destination with required follow-up in place
jtbds.outcome: patient discharged with care transition plan, medication reconciliation complete, follow-up scheduled
success_criteria: discharge planning initiated within 24 hours of admission
medication reconciliation completed before discharge
follow-up appointment scheduled within 7 days for high-risk patients
30-day readmission rate < 10%

jtbds.title: Process and verify prescription
jtbds.situation: prescriber submits prescription (electronic or paper); pharmacist must verify patient identity, check interactions, confirm coverage, and dispense or counsel on alternatives
jtbds.motivation: ensure patient receives the correct medication at appropriate dose with no harmful interactions and with insurance coverage confirmed
jtbds.outcome: prescription dispensed or therapeutic alternative offered, patient counseled, PDMP checked
success_criteria: PDMP checked for all Schedule II-IV medications
drug interaction screening completed before dispensing
patient counseled on new medications
controlled substance verified against DEA number

jtbds.title: Enroll patient in clinical trial
jtbds.situation: patient meets eligibility criteria for an IRB-approved trial; coordinator must obtain informed consent, verify eligibility, randomize, and register in CTMS
jtbds.motivation: advance research while protecting patient rights, ensuring protocol compliance, and meeting enrollment targets
jtbds.outcome: patient enrolled, consent documented, baseline data collected, CTMS record created
success_criteria: informed consent obtained before any protocol procedures
eligibility confirmation documented in source records
subject randomization completed per protocol
FDA Form 1572 on file for IND studies
enrollment registered on ClinicalTrials.gov within 21 days

jtbds.title: Handle patient safety incident report
jtbds.situation: staff member reports an adverse event, near-miss, or sentinel event; risk manager must investigate, classify, conduct RCA if required, and implement corrective actions
jtbds.motivation: prevent recurrence of patient harm, meet Joint Commission sentinel event reporting obligations, and protect the organization from liability
jtbds.outcome: incident classified, root cause analysis completed, corrective action plan implemented and monitored
success_criteria: incident acknowledged within 1 hour of report
preliminary assessment completed within 24 hours
root cause analysis completed within 45 days for sentinel events
corrective action plan implemented and verified within 90 days
Joint Commission notified within 5 business days for sentinel events

jtbds.title: Process medical record request
jtbds.situation: patient, attorney, insurer, or government entity requests medical records; HIM must verify authorization, redact non-authorized information, and release within statutory timeframes
jtbds.motivation: fulfill the patient's right of access and legal obligations while protecting PHI and preventing unauthorized disclosure
jtbds.outcome: records released to authorized requester, access log updated, fees collected if applicable
success_criteria: valid authorization verified before any release
records provided within 30 days of request (HIPAA)
urgent requests fulfilled within 3 business days
minimum necessary standard applied to all releases
accounting of disclosures maintained for non-TPO releases

jtbds.title: Credential and privilege a clinical provider
jtbds.situation: new physician, NP, or PA must be credentialed before seeing patients: primary source verification of licenses, malpractice history, DEA, board certifications, and hospital privileges
jtbds.motivation: ensure only qualified, verified providers render care to patients
jtbds.outcome: credentials verified; privileges granted; provider active in scheduling and billing
success_criteria: all licenses verified through primary sources
malpractice history reviewed (past 5 years)
DEA registration verified
privileges granted per department-specific criteria
provider active in scheduling and billing systems

jtbds.title: Appeal insurance claim denial
jtbds.situation: payer denies a claim for medical necessity, coding, or authorization; provider must gather clinical documentation, draft appeal letter, submit within payer appeal window, and track through peer-to-peer and IRO if needed
jtbds.motivation: recover revenue for services legitimately rendered
jtbds.outcome: appeal submitted; denial overturned or final denial received
success_criteria: appeal filed within payer appeal deadline
clinical documentation attached
peer-to-peer requested within 24 hours of denial if appropriate
IRO referral submitted if second-level denied

jtbds.title: Manage inpatient bed capacity
jtbds.situation: hospital census approaching capacity; coordinator must identify discharge-ready patients, coordinate pending admissions, manage boarding in ED, and escalate to incident command if needed
jtbds.motivation: prevent patient harm from boarding and ensure ED throughput
jtbds.outcome: capacity managed within target; escalations documented; census report updated
success_criteria: discharge-ready patients identified by 10am daily
pending admissions matched to available beds within 2 hours
ED boarding time below target threshold
incident command activated if house-full

jtbds.title: Conduct concurrent utilization review
jtbds.situation: payer or internal UR nurse reviews inpatient stay daily to confirm continued medical necessity; must document clinical criteria met, communicate with attending, and initiate discharge planning if criteria no longer met
jtbds.motivation: ensure appropriate level of care while preventing avoidable days and denials
jtbds.outcome: UR determination documented; payer notified; discharge plan initiated if criteria not met
success_criteria: UR review completed daily for all inpatients
clinical criteria documented in UR software
attending notified if criteria not met
discharge plan initiated within 24 hours if criteria not met

jtbds.title: Manage medication reconciliation at transitions of care
jtbds.situation: patient transitions between care settings; complete medication list must be reconciled, discrepancies flagged, and patient/caregiver counseled
jtbds.motivation: prevent adverse drug events caused by medication discrepancies at care transitions
jtbds.outcome: reconciled medication list signed by provider; discrepancies resolved; patient counseled
success_criteria: best possible medication history completed within 24 hours of admission
discrepancies flagged and resolved before discharge
discharge medication list provided to patient
patient/caregiver counseled on medication changes

jtbds.title: Manage infectious disease outbreak response
jtbds.situation: cluster of HAIs or community outbreak detected; IP must identify cases, implement transmission-based precautions, notify public health, and lead root-cause investigation
jtbds.motivation: contain outbreak, protect patients and staff, and meet mandatory reporting requirements
jtbds.outcome: outbreak declared contained; root cause identified; corrective actions implemented; report filed
success_criteria: case identification and line list within 24 hours
transmission-based precautions implemented
public health notified within required timeframe
root-cause analysis completed within 30 days

jtbds.title: Conduct clinical peer review
jtbds.situation: quality event triggers peer review of a physician clinical decision making; committee must gather records, apply criteria, score, communicate findings, and maintain confidentiality under state peer review protection
jtbds.motivation: improve clinical quality while protecting peer review privilege
jtbds.outcome: peer review complete; action plan or no-action documented; credentials file updated
success_criteria: case selected and criteria applied consistently
peer reviewer blinded to outcome
findings communicated to physician
credentials file updated
peer review privilege maintained

jtbds.title: Manage value-based care contract performance
jtbds.situation: health system has ACO or bundled payment contracts with payers; quality director must track performance metrics, identify gaps, coordinate care management interventions, and report to payer at contract milestones
jtbds.motivation: earn shared savings or avoid penalties under value-based payment arrangements
jtbds.outcome: performance report submitted; gap closure interventions documented; shared savings calculated
success_criteria: quality metrics tracked monthly against contract targets
gap closure interventions documented per patient
performance report submitted on payer schedule
shared savings calculation verified

jtbds.title: Process organ donation authorization
jtbds.situation: brain-dead or DCD patient is a potential donor; OPO coordinator must approach family, obtain authorization, coordinate medical management to preserve organs, arrange surgical recovery, and facilitate allocation
jtbds.motivation: honor patient donation wishes and save lives of transplant recipients
jtbds.outcome: authorization obtained or declined; organs recovered and allocated; family bereavement support arranged
success_criteria: family approached within 1 hour of identification as potential donor
authorization documented
medical management maintained to preserve organ viability
UNOS allocation followed
family bereavement support offered

jtbds.title: Manage telehealth visit
jtbds.situation: patient scheduled for telehealth encounter requires technology setup, consent, identity verification, state licensure check for provider, and documentation in EHR with appropriate modifier for billing
jtbds.motivation: deliver accessible care remotely while maintaining quality and billing compliance
jtbds.outcome: telehealth visit completed; encounter documented; claim submitted with correct modifiers
success_criteria: patient technology verified before appointment
written telehealth consent obtained
provider state licensure confirmed
encounter documented in EHR
claim submitted with telehealth modifier

jtbds.title: Handle adverse event reporting (AHRQ PSO)
jtbds.situation: serious safety event requires internal review and voluntary external reporting to Patient Safety Organization; PSO work product privilege must be maintained while conducting root cause analysis and filing reports
jtbds.motivation: learn from adverse events to prevent recurrence while protected from discovery
jtbds.outcome: internal RCA complete; PSO report filed; corrective actions implemented
success_criteria: event reported to PSO within 45 days
RCA completed within 45 days
corrective actions documented
PSO privilege maintained throughout process

jtbds.title: Manage population health care gaps
jtbds.situation: health system or payer identifies cohort of patients with gaps in preventive care; outreach campaign must be designed, executed, responses tracked, and HEDIS measure closure documented
jtbds.motivation: close HEDIS and Stars measure gaps to improve quality scores and reimbursement
jtbds.outcome: outreach campaign sent; gap closure rate reported; HEDIS data submitted
success_criteria: patient cohort correctly identified
outreach campaign executed before measurement period end
gap closure documented in EHR
HEDIS data submission accurate

jtbds.title: Process home health authorization and start of care
jtbds.situation: physician orders home health; coordinator must verify Medicare/Medicaid eligibility, obtain prior authorization, schedule initial visit, complete OASIS assessment, and establish Plan of Care within mandated timeframe
jtbds.motivation: initiate covered home health services quickly to support safe discharge and prevent readmission
jtbds.outcome: authorization confirmed; OASIS filed; POC signed by physician; services started
success_criteria: eligibility verified before start of care
PA obtained from payer
initial visit scheduled within 48 hours of discharge
OASIS completed within 5 days
POC signed within 30 days

jtbds.title: Manage durable medical equipment order
jtbds.situation: physician orders DME; coordinator must verify insurance coverage, obtain CMN, arrange delivery, collect patient signatures, and submit claim with correct HCPCS codes
jtbds.motivation: ensure patient receives necessary equipment quickly and claim is paid
jtbds.outcome: DME delivered; CMN on file; claim submitted and paid
success_criteria: coverage verified before order placed
CMN obtained from physician
equipment delivered within 3 business days
claim submitted with correct HCPCS codes

jtbds.title: Conduct clinical documentation improvement audit
jtbds.situation: CDI specialist queries medical record to ensure diagnoses are captured at highest specificity for DRG assignment; physician must respond to queries within 24 hours; final coded record must reflect clinical complexity
jtbds.motivation: ensure accurate risk-adjusted reimbursement and quality scores by capturing true clinical severity
jtbds.outcome: queries resolved; diagnosis list updated; DRG finalized; claim submitted
success_criteria: query sent within 24 hours of admission
physician response within 24 hours of query
diagnosis updated to highest specificity
DRG reflects clinical complexity
claim submitted with final codes

jtbds.title: Process pharmacy benefit prior authorization
jtbds.situation: specialty drug requires prior authorization from PBM; pharmacist or PA coordinator must gather clinical notes, submit via CoverMyMeds or payer portal, respond to peer-to-peer requests, and obtain approval or denial
jtbds.motivation: obtain coverage for medically necessary specialty medications within therapy start timelines
jtbds.outcome: PA approved or final denial issued; patient enrolled in hub or alternative pathway
success_criteria: PA submitted within 24 hours of prescription
peer-to-peer conducted if initial denial
approval obtained before specialty pharmacy dispenses
patient enrolled in manufacturer hub if approved

jtbds.title: Manage outpatient surgery scheduling and pre-op
jtbds.situation: surgeon schedules outpatient procedure; coordinator must verify insurance, obtain authorization, schedule anesthesia and OR time, complete pre-op H&P and lab orders, and provide patient with arrival instructions
jtbds.motivation: ensure surgery proceeds on schedule without authorization or clinical gaps
jtbds.outcome: surgery scheduled; authorization confirmed; pre-op complete; patient instructed
success_criteria: insurance verified and PA obtained before surgery date
OR and anesthesia scheduled
H&P completed within 30 days of surgery
pre-op labs resulted
patient instructions provided

jtbds.title: Handle patient grievance
jtbds.situation: patient files formal grievance about care quality, billing, or access; CMS CoPs require written acknowledgement within 7 days and resolution within 30 days; complex grievances require committee review
jtbds.motivation: resolve patient concerns fairly and demonstrate responsiveness to meet CMS grievance standards
jtbds.outcome: grievance acknowledged; investigation complete; written resolution delivered within CMS timeline
success_criteria: written acknowledgement within 7 days
investigation complete within 30 days
written resolution delivered
grievance log updated
regulatory agency notified if applicable

jtbds.title: Process 340B drug program purchase
jtbds.situation: covered entity must procure 340B-priced medications, maintain split-billing to prevent diversion, and pass HRSA audit without findings
jtbds.motivation: capture 340B savings while maintaining program compliance to avoid HRSA termination
jtbds.outcome: 340B purchases processed; inventory reconciled; audit trail complete
success_criteria: eligible patients identified before purchase
split-billing software reconciles 340B vs WAC inventory
no diversion or duplicate discount identified
HRSA audit documentation current

jtbds.title: Process advance directive and POLST
jtbds.situation: patient or family requests advance directive (living will, healthcare proxy) or POLST form; social worker must counsel patient, witness signatures, upload to EHR, notify care team, and ensure form follows patient across care settings
jtbds.motivation: honor patient care preferences at end of life and reduce unwanted aggressive interventions
jtbds.outcome: advance directive signed and scanned into EHR; care team notified; form available at all care sites
success_criteria: patient counseled on directive options
document witnessed per state requirements
uploaded to EHR and flagged in chart
care team notification sent
POLST on door or chart per policy
